story: Dolly Jameson’s precise mornings began to break: one day she woke late, missed her bus, was soaked, and learned she’d been put first on the quarterly report she’d built. In the restroom, her reflection smiled without her, and Dolly shut her eyes. She woke again at 6:15; ZoZo the cat spoke; the rain and five splashes returned; the mirror whispered, “Then close your eyes.” The next “morning,” the clock flickered nonsense; her phone claimed it was Wednesday. Already at the office, Mr. Banda reread Dolly’s slides and decided he’d gladly move her to later in the agenda if she asked; the shareholders wouldn’t mind a shuffle. His call came: the shareholders were waiting. Dolly blinked and stood before the projector, throat full of cotton, as everyone turned to the window.
implausible_knowledge: Already at the office, Mr. Banda reread Dolly’s slides and decided he’d gladly move her to later in the agenda if she asked; the shareholders wouldn’t mind a shuffle.
question: When Dolly suddenly finds herself in front of the projector with the shareholders waiting, what will she do?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask Mr. Banda to move her to a later slot so she can compose herself first.
answer_without_knowledge: Begin the presentation haltingly and try to push through despite her disorientation.
